Title: Registration Form - Personal View
Preconditions: Browser open; registration page accessible
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/sign-up
[ACT] Select 'Personal' account type if toggle present
[ASSERT] Verify form renders all fields
Assertions:
[DOM] 'First Name' input visible
[DOM] 'Last Name' input visible
[DOM] 'Email' input visible
[DOM] 'Password' input visible
[DOM] 'Confirm Password' input visible
[DOM] Terms/conditions checkbox present
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
[NETWORK] GET /sign-up returns HTTP 200
Failure_Signals: Spinner visible >5s; console TypeError; form fields missing after load

Title: Create Account - Personal User
Preconditions: Registration page open; unique test email available
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/sign-up
[ACT] Select 'Personal' account type
[ACT] Fill 'First Name': 'TestFirst'
[ACT] Fill 'Last Name': 'TestLast'
[ACT] Fill 'Email': testuser+{{timestamp}}@example.com
[ACT] Fill 'Password': {{USER_PASSWORD}}
[ACT] Fill 'Confirm Password': {{USER_PASSWORD}}
[ACT] Check terms/conditions checkbox
[ACT] Click 'Create Account'
[WAIT] Wait for success message or redirect
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/sign-in
[ACT] Fill 'Email': testuser+{{timestamp}}@example.com
[ACT] Fill 'Password': {{USER_PASSWORD}}
[ACT] Click 'Sign In'
Assertions:
[DOM] Success confirmation message visible after registration
[STATE] User redirected to account page or login page
[STATE] Login with new credentials succeeds
[STATE] User is authenticated after login
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] mutation errors[] is empty
[NETWORK] POST /connect/token returns HTTP 200 after login
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Spinner visible >5s; 4xx/5xx on registration endpoint; redirect to /error page; login fails after registration
Cleanup: Delete test account via admin after test

Title: Login on Behalf - Admin Initiated (Form Flow)
Preconditions: Admin (Administrator-type, back-office only) signed into Admin SPA; SUPPORT_AGENT (@td(SUPPORT_AGENT.email)) is a storefront-loginable user with platform:security:loginOnBehalf; IMPERSONATE_TARGET (@td(IMPERSONATE_TARGET.email)) exists in Security > Users; vcst-qa environment. NOTE: admin authenticates via admin back-office only — admin session is cross-origin to storefront and does NOT auto-propagate; the storefront form requires SUPPORT_AGENT credentials, not admin's.
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{ADMIN_URL}} and log in with {{ADMIN_EMAIL}} / {{ADMIN_PASSWORD}}
[NAV] Open Security > Users in Admin SPA (left navigation: Security → Users)
[ACT] Search for the target user @td(IMPERSONATE_TARGET.email) and open their user account detail blade
[ASSERT] Verify 'Login on behalf' button is present in the user account blade toolbar (key icon)
[ACT] Click 'Login on behalf' button in the user account blade toolbar
[WAIT] Wait for new storefront tab/window to open
[ASSERT] Verify new tab URL matches {{FRONT_URL}}/[locale/]account/impersonate/@td(IMPERSONATE_TARGET.userId) (locale prefix optional; public top-level route; no /sign-in redirect)
[ASSERT] Verify Security Verification Form is rendered (h1 'Security verification'; subtitle 'Confirm your identity to continue'; description 'You are logging in on behalf of a customer. The session will be audited.')
[ASSERT] Verify email and password inputs are visible and editable; 'Verify and continue' button visible (initially disabled until form is valid); 'Cancel' button visible
[ACT] Fill 'Email': @td(SUPPORT_AGENT.email) (storefront credentials of the support user — admin cannot sign in to storefront, only to back-office)
[ACT] Fill 'Password': @td(SUPPORT_AGENT.password)
[ACT] Click 'Verify and continue'
[WAIT] Wait for step transition verify → impersonate → success (loading state on submit button)
[ASSERT] Green success VcAlert visible with text 'Verification successful. Switching to customer view…'
[WAIT] Wait up to 3s for broadcast-triggered tab reload
[ASSERT] After reload: storefront header shows the impersonated identity (matches @td(IMPERSONATE_TARGET.name) / @td(IMPERSONATE_TARGET.email))
[ASSERT] Verify impersonation banner visible on storefront
Assertions:
[DOM] 'Login on behalf' button present in Security > User blade toolbar
[STATE] Admin click opens storefront /account/impersonate/{userId} in a new tab — admin session does NOT auto-propagate cross-origin
[DOM] Security Verification Form rendered (form flow — guest visitor; no silent skip)
[STATE] Successful credential submission → POST /connect/token grant_type=impersonate succeeds
[STATE] Step transitions: idle → verify → impersonate → success
[STATE] After broadcast reload: storefront session is the target user's session
[STATE] Customer-specific catalog; prices; and cart are visible
[STATE] Impersonation banner visible throughout browsing
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] POST /connect/token (verify): returns 200 with access_token for support admin
[API] POST /connect/token grant_type=impersonate; scope=offline_access; user_id={userId} returns HTTP 200 with access_token; refresh_token; expires_in; token_type
[NETWORK] No 4xx/5xx on either /connect/token call
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: 'Login on behalf' button missing from user blade toolbar; new tab does not open or opens to /sign-in (route not public); Security Verification Form not rendered; token exchange returns 4xx; storefront shows admin context after reload; broadcast does not trigger tab reload within 3s; console TypeError
Cleanup: Stop impersonation via banner button; logout admin from Admin SPA
References: regression-qa-theme-26.csv; Synced: VCST-4905 PR #2279 (2026-05-11); Revised after Phase 5 V4b — admin click opens form (NOT silent flow) because admin session is cross-origin to storefront; Revised again 2026-05-11 — form credentials must be SUPPORT_AGENT (storefront-loginable Customer-type) NOT admin (admin is back-office-only); Executed PASS 2026-05-11 v3 — admin SPA → Security > Users → David blade → 'Login on behalf' → new storefront tab → form rendered → John's creds entered → both /connect/token 200 → header shows 'John Mitchell logged in as David Kim' (evidence: reports/test-lifecycle/TLC-2026-05-11-2114/execution-evidence/auth-027-v3-john-impersonates-david-PASS-storefront-identity-switched.png)

Title: Login on Behalf - Org Maintainer (Form Flow)
Preconditions: Organization maintainer account (without StorefrontPermissions.CanImpersonate) logged into storefront; at least one member in same org; target member userId resolvable
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/sign-in
[ACT] Fill 'Email': {{ORG_USER_EMAIL}}
[ACT] Fill 'Password': {{ORG_USER_PASSWORD}}
[ACT] Click 'Sign In'
[WAIT] Wait for redirect to account area
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/company/members
[WAIT] Wait for members list to load
[ACT] Locate an employee member row and note their userId (from URL or member card)
[ACT] Click 'Login on Behalf' or equivalent member action
[WAIT] Wait for navigation to /account/impersonate/{memberId}
[ASSERT] Verify current URL matches {{FRONT_URL}}/account/impersonate/{memberId}
[ASSERT] Verify page title text 'Security verification' is rendered in an h1 element
[ASSERT] Verify subtitle 'Confirm your identity to continue' is visible
[ASSERT] Verify description 'You are logging in on behalf of a customer. The session will be audited.' is visible
[ASSERT] Verify email input (type=email) is present and empty
[ASSERT] Verify password input (type=password) is present and empty
[ASSERT] Verify 'Verify and continue' submit button is visible
[ASSERT] Verify 'Cancel' button (variant outline) is visible
[ACT] Fill email input: {{ORG_USER_EMAIL}}
[ACT] Fill password input: {{ORG_USER_PASSWORD}}
[ACT] Click 'Verify and continue' button
[WAIT] Wait for success state (green VcAlert or reload)
[ASSERT] After success: storefront session reflects the impersonated member's context (name/org in header)
[ASSERT] Verify impersonation banner is visible
[ACT] Attempt navigation to {{FRONT_URL}}/account/impersonate/@td(OTHER_ORG_USER.userId) (user from a different org; if alias exists)
[ASSERT] Impersonation of outside-org user is denied (401/403 from /connect/token or error state shown)
Assertions:
[DOM] ImpersonateForm rendered: h1 'Security verification'; subtitle; description; email input; password input; 'Verify and continue' button; 'Cancel' button
[STATE] Form flow triggered — org maintainer without CanImpersonate sees Security Verification Form
[STATE] Valid credentials in form → impersonate token obtained → storefront switches to member context
[STATE] Impersonation limited to own organization members; outside-org targets rejected
[STATE] Impersonation banner visible after successful switch
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] POST /connect/token with grant_type=impersonate returns HTTP 200 with access_token for own-org member
[NETWORK] /connect/token impersonate grant returns 401 or 403 for outside-org target
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Form not rendered (silent flow triggered unexpectedly); h1 or subtitle text missing; form submits without credentials; outside-org impersonation succeeds; impersonation banner absent after switch; console TypeError
Cleanup: Stop impersonation via banner button; log out storefront user

Title: Security Verification Form — Support Agent Successfully Impersonates Target Customer
Preconditions: No active storefront session; SUPPORT_AGENT (@td(SUPPORT_AGENT.email)) can sign in to B2B-store via storefront /connect/token AND holds platform `platform:security:loginOnBehalf` (vc-platform PlatformConstants.SecurityLoginOnBehalf); target IMPERSONATE_TARGET (@td(IMPERSONATE_TARGET.email)) exists in Security > Users. VERIFIED 2026-05-11 — USR-001 John Mitchell satisfies both conditions through his role assignment on vcst-qa; impersonating David Kim (USR-007, different org ORG-002) succeeds end-to-end.
Steps:
[PRE:SIGNOUT]
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/account/impersonate/@td(IMPERSONATE_TARGET.userId)
[WAIT] Wait for ImpersonateForm to render
[ASSERT] Verify 'Verify and continue' button is disabled (meta.valid=false on empty form)
[ACT] Fill email input: @td(SUPPORT_AGENT.email) (support agent identity — John Mitchell)
[ACT] Fill password input: @td(SUPPORT_AGENT.password)
[ASSERT] Verify 'Verify and continue' button becomes enabled (meta.valid=true)
[ACT] Click 'Verify and continue' button
[WAIT] Wait for step to transition through verify → impersonate phases (loading state visible)
[ASSERT] Verify green VcAlert (success color) appears with text 'Verification successful. Switching to customer view…'
[WAIT] Wait up to 3s for broadcast-triggered tab reload
[ASSERT] After reload: storefront header reflects the impersonated TARGET identity (David Kim @td(IMPERSONATE_TARGET.name); not John Mitchell)
[ASSERT] Verify impersonation banner is visible on the resulting page
Assertions:
[STATE] Steps transition: idle → verify (loading) → impersonate → success
[DOM] Green success VcAlert shown: 'Verification successful. Switching to customer view…'
[STATE] After broadcast reload: session is the TARGET user's session (David Kim — not John Mitchell)
[STATE] Impersonation banner visible after reload
[STATE] Customer-specific context loaded (David's catalog/prices/cart)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] POST /connect/token (verify): returns 200 with access_token for John Mitchell @td(SUPPORT_AGENT.email)
[API] POST /connect/token grant_type=impersonate; scope=offline_access; user_id=@td(IMPERSONATE_TARGET.userId) returns HTTP 200 with access_token; refresh_token; expires_in; token_type (requires SUPPORT_AGENT to have StorefrontPermissions.CanImpersonate)
[NETWORK] No 4xx/5xx on either /connect/token call
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Success VcAlert not shown; page does not reload after 3s; storefront session still shows John identity; impersonation banner absent; token exchange returns 4xx; console TypeError
Cleanup: Stop impersonation via banner button; navigate to {{FRONT_URL}} to restore neutral state
References: VCST-4905; PR #2279; Synced: VCST-4905 PR #2279 (2026-05-11); Scenario corrected 2026-05-11: support agent = John Mitchell (SUPPORT_AGENT), target = David Kim (IMPERSONATE_TARGET); Executed PASS 2026-05-11 v2 — both /connect/token calls returned 200, storefront switched into David's session, header shows 'John Mitchell logged in as David Kim' (evidence: reports/test-lifecycle/TLC-2026-05-11-2114/execution-evidence/auth-070-v2-john-david-PASS-impersonation-success.png)
